title: Red Dead Redemption 2

notes:
I have been using Linux Desktop for years, and I have never experienced such an issue before. A Wine/Proton program should never be able to crash the desktop operating system. Other games and programs run fine.

I launched RRD2 years ago, and it was perfectly fine. I'm sure that I will find a workaround, or the core issue will be addressed eventually, but this is a critical bug.

At first I assumed it was the typical Nvidia driver and Wayland issue, but I heard someone who had a similar experience with Linux Mint (X11) and an Nvidia GPU. I found it hard to believe until I reproduced a similar issue myself without knowing.

I don't know the root cause yet. I will send another report but this issue needs more attention. I can confirm it's not from my end or own configuration or a skill issue. I was testing Bazzite, fresh install, with configuration, installed RRD2 to test the Nvidia and Wayland situtation, and it happened.

The end of my previous system log:

title: Red Dead Redemption 2

notes:
Same as my last report. I was able to launch the game after some tweaks on my last installation, but out of curiosity, I wanted to see whether this bug is reproducible on Cachy OS.

The same crash was reproduced, except this time I was not able to view the initial menu.

I'm using NVIDIA open-source kernel modules, Linux 7.0.0-1-cachyos, Nvidia 595.58.03, KDE DE, and CachyOS.

Last time I reproduced the issue on Linux 6.17.7-ba29.fc43.x86_64, NVIDIA proprietary 595.58.03, Bazzite, Gnome DE (while changing the graphics settings or loading the story mode).

I'm not sure if I can provide reproducible steps. It could be a hardware issue since I couldn't find anyone who experienced a similar issue on ProtonDB, but this is the only game that crashed the Linux desktop via Proton.
